question: What is the average current salary per department? Use each employee's most recent salary record. Show department name and average, highest first.

attempts.raw_response: {"sql": "WITH latest AS (SELECT s.employee_id, s.amount FROM salaries AS s JOIN (SELECT employee_id, MAX(effective_date) AS max_date FROM salaries GROUP BY employee_id) AS m ON s.employee_id = m.employee_id AND s.effective_date = m.max_date) SELECT d.name, ROUND(AVG(latest.amount), 2) AS avg_salary FROM latest JOIN employees AS e ON latest.employee_id = e.employee_id JOIN departments AS d ON e.department_id = d.department_id GROUP BY d.department_id, d.name ORDER BY avg_salary DESC, d.name", "assumptions": ["gold replay"], "selected_tables": []}

candidate.sql: WITH latest AS (SELECT s.employee_id, s.amount FROM salaries AS s JOIN (SELECT employee_id, MAX(effective_date) AS max_date FROM salaries GROUP BY employee_id) AS m ON s.employee_id = m.employee_id AND s.effective_date = m.max_date) SELECT d.name, ROUND(AVG(latest.amount), 2) AS avg_salary FROM latest JOIN employees AS e ON latest.employee_id = e.employee_id JOIN departments AS d ON e.department_id = d.department_id GROUP BY d.department_id, d.name ORDER BY avg_salary DESC, d.name

policy.normalized_sql: WITH latest AS (SELECT s.employee_id, s.amount FROM salaries AS s JOIN (SELECT employee_id, MAX(effective_date) AS max_date FROM salaries GROUP BY employee_id) AS m ON s.employee_id = m.employee_id AND s.effective_date = m.max_date) SELECT d.name, ROUND(AVG(latest.amount), 2) AS avg_salary FROM latest JOIN employees AS e ON latest.employee_id = e.employee_id JOIN departments AS d ON e.department_id = d.department_id GROUP BY d.department_id, d.name ORDER BY avg_salary DESC, d.name LIMIT 200
